links.targetTitle: Bee Reactor Adapter ships two npm packages: adapter and connect fork
A Powerhouse project has four main directories: document-models, editors, processors, and subgraphs

global.title: bee-reactor-adaptor is a pnpm monorepo of two packages: @liberuum-org/bee-reactor-adapter and @liberuum-org/connect
global.content:
# Two-Package Monorepo Structure

The `bee-reactor-adaptor` repository is a pnpm monorepo with two publishable npm packages that split responsibilities between the core adapter library and the forked Connect host app.

| Package | npm name | Role |
|---------|----------|------|
| `packages/adapter/` | `@liberuum-org/bee-reactor-adapter` | Core adapter: SwarmClient, SwarmChannel, encryption, feeds, stamps |
| `packages/connect/` | `@liberuum-org/connect` | Fork of Connect with Swarm landing page + settings UI |

The adapter package is framework-agnostic and can in principle integrate into any Reactor-based host. The connect package is a Connect fork that consumes it and adds the Swarm-specific landing page plus settings UI (stamp management, storage stats, sync badges, folder tree, USD pricing).

Development uses `pnpm install` at the repo root, `pnpm build` to compile both, and `pnpm test` to run the 12-test E2E suite against a live `bee dev` node.
global.description: Monorepo splits into the framework-agnostic adapter (SwarmClient, SwarmChannel, encryption, feeds, stamps) and a Connect fork consuming it with a Swarm landing page and settings UI.
